title: What We Can Learn From Neonatal Database Research
authors: Veeral Nalin Tolia, Reese H. Clark, and Karna Murthy

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ neonatal database research?
ทุกวันที่ทำงานใน NICU เราตัดสินใจโดยอิงจาก evidence — แต่ evidence นั้นมาจากไหน? มากกว่า 82 neonatal clinical databases ทั่วโลก ที่ผลิต >2,000 publications สร้าง evidence ที่เราใช้วันนี้. บทนี้สอน 5 สิ่ง: (1) database research ทำอะไรได้ที่ RCT ทำไม่ได้ — disease incidence, practice variation, adverse event detection, risk estimators, comparative effectiveness; (2) bias 4 ประเภทหลัก ที่ต้องรู้เพื่อตีความผลการศึกษาอย่างถูกต้อง; (3) เหตุใด 'statistically significant' ≠ 'clinically meaningful' ในการศึกษา big data; (4) denominator และ numerator definition ที่ชัดเจนคือ foundation ของการวิเคราะห์ที่ถูกต้อง; (5) อนาคต: machine learning, target trials, real-time dashboards จะเปลี่ยน neonatology อย่างไร.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
ระบุ major neonatal databases: VON, NICHD NRN, Pediatrix CDW, CHNC, CPQCC, PHIS, CNN, UK NNRD — และ scope ของแต่ละ database
อธิบาย 6 domains ที่ database research ตอบได้: disease definition, practice variation, adverse events, risk estimators, questions unsuited for RCTs, comparative effectiveness
แยกแยะ bias 4 ประเภท: ascertainment bias, selection bias, referral bias, survivor bias — พร้อมตัวอย่างจริงใน neonatology
เข้าใจปัญหา denominator definition และ numerator definition ในการตีความ database study
อธิบาย statistical methods ที่ใช้บ่อย: logistic regression, Cochran-Armitage test, propensity score matching, matching
อธิบาย big data paradox: sample ใหญ่ → ง่ายต่อการ detect statistically significant differences ที่ไม่ clinically meaningful
เปรียบเทียบ data sources: patient registry (manual abstraction) vs electronic/administrative data — ข้อดีข้อเสียแต่ละแบบ
รู้จัก STROBE guidelines และ directed acyclic graphs — ใช้ทำอะไรใน observational study
อธิบาย future directions: machine learning, target trials, real-time dashboards, wearables ใน NICU
ใช้ความรู้นี้ evaluate database publications ที่อ่านใน journal ได้อย่างมีวิจารณญาณ
1. Neonatal Database Research — Overview
1.1 Scale and Scope

Systematic review (Statnikov et al. Arch Dis Child 2017;102:F270): identified >82 neonatal clinical databases worldwide — both regional and national, from many countries. These databases have produced >2,000 publications. Big data describes large, complex data that are both structured (predefined: ICD codes, GA, BW) and unstructured (free text, pictures, audio). Data scientists spend 50–80% of their time curating and preparing data before analysis.

Database Research ≠ RCT — แต่ Complement กัน
RCT: prospectively designed, randomized, minimizes bias, establishes cause-and-effect — แต่ generalizability จำกัด, enrollment ยาก, ราคาแพง, ไม่เหมาะกับ rare diseases หรือ ethical constraints. Database research: real-world data, large N, generalizability สูง, hypothesis-generating — แต่ observational = report associations only, ไม่ establish causation, prone to confounding by indication. สรุป: database studies = best used as hypothesis-generating tools + จาก RCT สู่ real-world evidence.
2. Major Neonatal Databases — รู้จักแต่ละอัน

rows: 401–1500g หรือ 22–29+6 wk; key outcomes: mortality, IVH ≥III, NEC, LOS, BPD, hypothermia on admission, BF at discharge
Edwards et al. Transl Pediatr 2019;8:182 — worldwide learning community
VLBW/ELBW; RCTs + observational; landmark trials (SUPPORT, BOOST II, COT, SCAMP, ibuprofen PDA)
All NICU admissions to Pediatrix-managed NICUs; very large N; captures drugs/diagnoses
Tolia VN, Clark RH — cefotaxime mortality signal; NAS rising incidence
Complex, referred patients; CDH, severe BPD, NEC surgery, HIE; structured data with 3 participation levels
Murthy K et al. — CHND schema (Fig 3.2)
All California NICUs; QI + outcomes; racial/ethnic diversity; links birth certificates
Ngo et al. Pediatrics 2017 — PDA management trends
Administrative/billing data; ≥44 children's hospitals; nephrotoxins, CDH iNO, costs
Mohamed et al.: 74% neonates exposed ≥1 nephrotoxic drug
VLBW; outcomes + QI; racial disparity data
Lokku et al. Am J Perinatol 2017 — PDA trends Canada
All NICU admissions UK; linked EHR data
Gale et al. Arch Dis Child 2016;101:216

blocks.text: CDC National Vital Statistics System: birth + death certificates; population-level epidemiology; links ใน birth certificate–hospital discharge analyses
Neonatal Research Network of Japan: VLBW outcomes; congenital anomaly rates (Kawasaki et al. J Pediatr 2020;226:106)
3. 6 Domains ที่ Database Research ตอบได้
3.1 Defining Neonatal Diseases — Incidence, Emerging Diseases, Standards
Reporting incidence of neonatal morbidities ใน extremely preterm infants: NICHD NRN → Bell et al. JAMA 2022 — outcomes of 10,877 infants 22–28 wk born 2013–2018 at 19 NRN centers

Tracking emerging diseases + cost: NAS (neonatal abstinence syndrome) — Tolia VN et al. NEJM 2015;372:2118: NAS in US NICUs increased 3.4-fold from 2004–2013; average hospital charges increased from $39,400 → $66,700
Distinguishing SIP vs NEC: Attridge et al. J Perinatol 2006 — SIP associated with early indomethacin; different from NEC; database studies discriminated these when each is uncommon individually
Growth standards: Fenton growth chart, Olsen intrauterine growth curves — developed from database analyses of large birth weight + GA datasets
Disease-specific: fetal growth curves สำหรับ infants with gastroschisis developed + described ด้วย database data
3.2 Understanding Variations in Care — Practice Variation

Practice Variation ที่ใหญ่ที่สุด — 22–24 wk Resuscitation
Intercenter variability in initiation and active treatment of infants born at 22, 23, and 24 wk — documented ใน NICHD NRN registry. ผลที่ตามมา: ข้อมูลนี้ shift thresholds ของ clinical + ethical decision making at limits of perinatal viability. Centers ที่ treat มี survival สูงกว่า → ไม่ใช่ biological but practice difference. Database research = เครื่องมือหลักในการเปิดเผย variation และกระตุ้น standardization.
PDA management trends: marked decline in medical/surgical treatment ใน VLBW — conservative management increasing; documented across VON, CPQCC (Ngo et al.), CNN (Lokku et al.)
Steroid use + BPD: AAP consensus statement on postnatal steroids → database tracking showed dramatic ↓ postnatal dexamethasone use → สัมพันธ์กับ ↓ BPD rate
Therapeutic hypothermia: therapeutic creep — increasing use in populations outside original trial criteria (younger GA, milder HIE) — detected ด้วย database surveillance (ref 27)
NICU rotation duration: database study (ref 42) — few differences in outcomes by rotation duration; only large N database could answer this administrative–clinical question
3.3 Detecting Adverse Events — Database as Pharmacovigilance

rows: NICHD NRN VLBW registry (Guillet et al.)
AAP: discouraged H2 blockers in preterm infants
Cefotaxime → ↑ mortality across all GA groups (incl. fungal sepsis ↑)
QI: cefotaxime ↓ → improved survival + ↓ fungal sepsis
74% of 192,229 neonates exposed ≥1 nephrotoxic drug → ↑ AKI
Risk factors for neonatal thromboses identified

blocks.text: Cefotaxime + fungal sepsis: Fig 3.1 ของ chapter แสดง cefotaxime ↓ dramatically ใน early 2000s หลัง signal + antibiotic stewardship QI — database tracking ยืนยันผลการ intervention
Late H2 blockers/steroids + BPD: confounding by indication ต้องพิจารณา — สตีรอยด์ให้หลังเพราะ BPD แย่อยู่แล้ว ไม่ใช่สตีรอยด์ทำให้ BPD แย่
3.4 Development of Risk Estimators

NICHD Outcome Estimator: calculates in-hospital + 2-year outcome risk for extremely preterm infants based on: GA, BW, sex, singleton/multiple, antenatal steroids (yes/no). Built from large NRN registry N → high precision for rare outcomes at extreme prematurity

EOS (Early-Onset Sepsis) Calculator: developed from Kaiser Permanente Northern California data — 200,000 newborns ≥35 wk; multivariable risk prediction → safely reduced lab testing + empiric antibiotics without increased missed EOS
Risk calculators for future BPD + sample size calculations for prospective trials = additional uses of database-derived risk estimators
3.5 Clinical Questions NOT Suited for RCTs
Vitamin A supply shortage (2011): national shortage → ↓ vitamin A use dramatically → BPD rates remained unchanged — natural experiment; RCT not feasible; database provided answer
Hydrocortisone dosing: higher doses → greater risk of death (ref 41) — impossible to RCT because consent/ethics; database necessary
Equity of care: racial/ethnic disparities in care delivery cannot be randomly assigned → only database can describe
NICU rotation duration, outborn admission impact on outcomes — 'clinical-administrative' questions where database = only way to get adequate sample size
3.6 Comparative Effectiveness Research

CDH + inhaled nitric oxide (iNO): PHIS database — 1,713 CDH infants at 33 US hospitals; >50% received iNO; iNO use varied dramatically between hospitals (practice variation); $81 million pharmacy charges; no correlation between iNO use and mortality or ECMO utilization

Surfactant preparations: no head-to-head RCT comparing 3 US preparations (cost + enrollment barriers); comparative effectiveness analysis or meta-analysis justified — database provides real-world evidence
3.7 Linkage Analyses

HIE + cost variation: linking clinical database (disease definitions + outcomes) + administrative database (charges + resource use) → 29% of total cost variations between centers for HIE identified — sources of variation revealed by linked data

Pulse oximetry alarm policy change: large RCTs suggested need for policy change → database analysis confirmed: policy change unrelated to mortality and severe ROP ใน very preterm infants — real-world effectiveness ≠ RCT expectation
4. การตีความ Database Studies — Bias 4 ประเภท

4 Types of Bias ที่ต้องรู้ — ท่องจำและใช้ในการ Journal Club
1. Ascertainment bias: systematic differences ใน subject definition/identification (e.g., SIP poorly defined ก่อน 1990s → apparent ↑ incidence เกิดจาก education not true disease increase). 2. Selection bias: measured/unmeasured differences between comparison groups (e.g., iNO appears to increase mortality because given to sickest infants — confounding by indication). 3. Referral bias: sample ≠ general population (e.g., quaternary NICUs have higher % congenital anomalies + NEC requiring surgery → outcomes not generalizable to community NICUs). 4. Survivor bias: early-use group includes infants who died before late-use group could receive drug (e.g., Dobson et al.: early caffeine appeared higher mortality at <24 wk — but late caffeine group had already survived early high-risk period).
4.1 Ascertainment Bias — Disease Definition Changes Over Time

SIP (spontaneous intestinal perforation) example: poorly defined late 1980s → appeared to ↑ as awareness grew and distinction from NEC clarified — change in coding/education, NOT change in disease biology
PDA coding: Bixler et al. found 25% reduction in PDA diagnosis during study period → almost certainly changing clinician diagnostic coding preferences, not true ↓ in PDA physiology
BPD ICD coding may not reflect actual respiratory disease severity; clinical sepsis has no ICD-10 code — ทำให้ tracking ยาก
4.2 Selection Bias + Confounding by Indication
iNO example: database studies show iNO → ↑ mortality without severity adjustment; แต่ iNO ให้เฉพาะ sickest infants (PPHN) → iNO = surrogate for severity, ไม่ใช่ cause of death
Confounding by indication: เมื่อ indication for treatment คือ severity ของ disease นั้นเอง → association ระหว่าง drug กับ poor outcome อาจ represent confounding ไม่ใช่ causation
4.3 Referral Bias — NICU Level of Care Matters
Quaternary/referral NICUs: higher % outborn, congenital anomalies, surgical NEC, complex disease → outcomes worse ≠ worse quality of care
Community NICU with delivery service: refers complex infants out → lower morbidity rate → comparisons invalid without level-of-care stratification

NEC example: infants with surgical NEC transferred to centers with pediatric surgery → comparing NEC outcomes between NICUs with/without surgical support = invalid without correction for referral bias
4.4 Survivor Bias — Early vs Late Treatment Timing

Classic example — Dobson et al. early caffeine: early caffeine group (<24 wk) had higher mortality — survival bias: infant in late caffeine group (received caffeine Day 5) had already survived the early high-mortality period; infant in early caffeine group was at risk throughout. The difference is artificial selection, not drug effect.
5. Understanding Data Sources — Registry vs Administrative

rows: High accuracy, reproducible, inter-rater reliability testable, pre-specified definitions
Volume: very large N; structured + unstructured; cost-effective; timeliness
Resource-intensive; slow; limited variable set; only captures what was designed to capture
Needs curation; coding errors; billing codes ≠ clinical reality; less accurate disease definitions
VON, NICHD NRN, CNN, CHNC, CPQCC
PHIS, CMS data, NIS (Nationwide Inpatient Sample)
Accurate outcome rates, quality metrics, risk-adjusted benchmarking
Cost/charges, utilization patterns, rare disease epidemiology, large-scale screening

blocks.text: 6. Denominator + Numerator Definition — Foundation ของ Study Validity
6.1 Denominator Definition — Who Is Eligible?
Key choices: include outborn referrals? exclude congenital anomalies? include transfers-out? — each choice meaningfully influences the denominator
Therapeutic hypothermia example: many HIE infants are outborn (transferred in) — must include outborn to accurately assess event rates. Excluding outborn → artificially low HIE rates
Transfers-out: infants transferred before developing late morbidities (BPD, ROP, NEC) contribute to denominator but not numerator → artificially low morbidity rates
Missing data: analyze included vs omitted infants → assess whether omission creates systematic bias
6.2 Numerator Definition — Disease Definition Precision
NEC vs SIP: clinically difficult to distinguish → NEC rate depends heavily on how well SIP is recognized and separately coded
PDA: Bixler et al. — 25% ↓ PDA coding over time → coding change, not biology change; ไม่สามารถ interpret as ↓ disease incidence
Suspected sepsis: real clinical entity but no standardized ICD-10 code — nearly impossible to track accurately in administrative databases
ICD-9 → ICD-10 transition: changes in coding structure → apparent changes in disease rates that are coding artifacts, not clinical reality
7. Statistical Methods ที่ใช้บ่อยใน Database Research
7.1 Monitoring Trends Over Time
Cochran-Armitage test: chi-square based — tests linearity between response variable and experimental variables; detects trends not noticed by crude methods
Linear regression: evaluates slope over time; significant if slope ≠ 0. Challenge: not all changes are linear → nonlinear models needed
For narrative/graphical reporting of therapy changes based on new evidence: annotated unadjusted graphs preferred over statistical tests (e.g., Fig 3.1: cefotaxime decline after QI)
7.2 Logistic Regression
Most common method ใน database studies; evaluates association between confounders and a dichotomous outcome (e.g., lived/died)

Table 2 Fallacy: misinterpreting effect estimates for covariates that were NOT the main exposure — common error; clinicians must focus on the primary exposure variable, not all covariates in the model
Large N → validation cohort: development cohort builds model → validation cohort tests prediction fidelity → foundation for risk adjustment
7.3 Propensity Score Matching
First: regression calculates probability that each infant receives a specific therapy (propensity score); matched groups have similar propensity scores → mimics randomization + reduces selection bias
Useful when RCT is: difficult to perform, needs equipoise establishment, requires large N for rare outcomes, or ethical constraints exist
7.4 Big Data Paradox — Statistical vs Clinical Significance

คำเตือนสำคัญ: Large N → ง่ายต่อ Detect Statistically Significant แต่ Clinically Trivial Differences
NICHD study example: maternal age differed significantly across GA groups — statistically significant แต่ 1-year difference in maternal age ไม่มี clinical significance. BW/HC example (Bloom et al.): QI project → HC difference 0.2 cm — statistically significant (p<0.05) but within measurement error and clinically meaningless. Rule: ใน big data always evaluate clinical significance และ effect size ไม่ใช่แค่ p-value.
8. อนาคตของ Big Data ใน Neonatology
8.1 New Data Streams ใน NICU
Continuous bedside monitoring: real-time HR, SpO₂, RR, BP — 1 preterm infant generates >1,000,000 data points/day; ventilator trends, NIRS (cerebral oxygenation) — กำลัง integrate เข้า database
Wearable devices: next-generation sensors expanding continuous physiological data capture beyond traditional NICU monitoring
Nursing + RT documentation: pain assessments, suction, repositioning, FiO₂ titration — underutilized but potentially valuable data source
8.2 Machine Learning + Target Trials
Machine learning: pattern recognition ใน high-dimensional data → early prediction of sepsis, NEC, BPD, IVH from bedside monitor streams + clinical data
Target trial framework: emulating RCT design using observational data — reduces bias from unmeasured confounding; increasingly accepted in neonatology (ref 76)
Real-time dashboards: monitoring changes in care + associated outcome changes in real-time → identify important associations as they emerge without waiting for annual reports
Database research augmenting future trials: identify high-risk populations, decrease sample sizes, reduce time + cost of clinical trials

ปรัชญาจาก Chapter: Knowledge ≠ Wisdom
'There is a difference between knowledge and wisdom. Knowledge is the accumulation of meaningful facts. Wisdom is integration of knowledge in a way that offers benefits.' — Tolia, Clark & Murthy 2025. Neonatal database research increases both diagnostic precision and therapeutic effectiveness — แต่ neonatologist ต้องอ่าน data ด้วย wisdom ไม่ใช่แค่ accept findings ของ big data studies โดยไม่ evaluate bias + clinical context.
9. STROBE Guidelines and Directed Acyclic Graphs

STROBE (Strengthening the Reporting of Observational Studies in Epidemiology): checklist ที่ช่วย evaluate completeness and transparency ของ observational studies — covers: title, abstract, introduction, methods (participants, variables, bias, statistical methods), results, discussion

Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs): visual diagrams แสดง causal pathways — ช่วย identify confounders, mediators, colliders ใน study design และ interpretation เมื่อ causal pathways ซับซ้อน; standard ใน modern epidemiology
Key point: association ≠ causation ใน observational studies — STROBE + DAGs help make limitations explicit
10. NICU Practical — อ่าน Database Study อย่างวิจารณ์
Step 1 — ระบุ data source: registry (VON/NRN) vs administrative (PHIS) — ช่วย evaluate accuracy of disease definitions และ clinical relevance
Step 2 — ตรวจ denominator: outborn included? anomalies excluded? transfers-out counted? Tolia & Clark 2020: 'The denominator matters!'
Step 3 — หา bias: ถามตัวเอง: is this confounding by indication? is there referral bias? is there survivor bias? เปลี่ยน ตีความไหม?
Step 4 — clinical significance: p < 0.05 ใน big data ≠ clinically important; ดู effect size + absolute risk difference + NNT
Step 5 — จาก association → action: database study ≠ enough to change practice alone; ต้องการ RCT, quasi-experimental, or strong QI evidence ก่อน implement practice change

สำหรับ KCMH: Thailand ยังไม่มี national NICU database ระดับ VON; NICHD outcome estimator ใช้ได้ใน Thai context — calibrate สำหรับ Thai population ก่อน; iNeo/CNN data = relevant baseline แต่ต้องระวัง referral + ascertainment bias เมื่อเปรียบเทียบกับ Thai outcomes
11. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Database ≠ RCT แต่ ทำได้สิ่งที่ RCT ทำไม่ได้
RCT = cause and effect แต่ limited sample + selected patients + expensive. Database = real world, large N, diverse population, hypothesis-generating — but association only. ทั้งสองสำคัญ: database generates hypothesis → RCT confirms causation. ถ้ารู้ข้อดี/ข้อจำกัดของแต่ละอย่าง จะ design better research และ interpret literature ได้ถูกต้อง.

PEARL 2 — Survivor Bias ใน Caffeine: 'Early Use = More Deaths' คือ Artifact
Dobson et al.: infants in 'late caffeine' group ได้รับ caffeine Day 5 — meaning they had ALREADY survived the high-mortality period. Infants in 'early caffeine' group were at risk throughout Day 1–5. The apparent difference is selection artifact, not drug effect. ทุกครั้งที่เห็น 'early vs late treatment' comparison ใน database study → think survivor bias.

PEARL 3 — Cefotaxime Signal: Database Detected, QI Fixed It
Pediatrix CDW signal: cefotaxime → ↑ mortality including ↑ fungal sepsis (Clark et al.). QI effort ↓ cefotaxime use → improved survival + ↓ fungal sepsis. Fig 3.1: dramatic decline after 2000. นี่คือ perfect example of database → adverse signal → QI action → outcome improvement. H2 blockers → NEC เป็น similar story.

PEARL 4 — Statistical Significance ≠ Clinical Meaning ใน Big Data
Sample size ใหญ่ → p<0.05 ง่ายมาก. Bloom et al.: HC +0.2 cm = statistically significant but within measurement error. NICHD: 1-year maternal age difference = statistically significant but clinically irrelevant. Always ask: What is the absolute difference? What is the NNT? Is this effect size large enough to change my practice?

PEARL 5 — 'The Denominator Matters!' — Tolia & Clark 2020
How denominator is defined determines everything. Include outborn = higher HIE N (correct). Exclude transfers-out from BPD/ROP denominator = artificially low rates. Exclude anomalies = lower mortality = favorable outcome but not representative of real NICU. ทุกครั้งที่อ่าน database study: identify denominator → assess inclusion/exclusion → judge generalizability.
12. Evidence Update — Key References จาก Chapter

Statnikov Y et al. Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed. 2017;102:F270: systematic review identified >82 neonatal clinical databases worldwide — ≥2,000 publications. Foundation reference for scope of neonatal database research.

Bell EF et al. (NICHD NRN). JAMA 2022;327:248: outcomes of 10,877 extremely preterm infants 22–28 wk born 2013–2018 at 19 NRN centers — landmark NRN registry study; rates of survival, major morbidities, care practices over time.

Tolia VN et al. NEJM 2015;372:2118: NAS rising incidence in US NICUs 2004–2013: 3.4-fold increase; hospital charges ↑ $39,400 → $66,700; documented via Pediatrix CDW — emerging disease signal via database.

Horbar JD et al. JAMA Pediatr 2017;171(3):e164396: variation in performance of NICUs in US across 743 VON centers — wide variation unexplained by case mix; foundation for quality improvement initiatives.
Patrick SW et al. JAMA 2012;307:1934: NAS + healthcare expenditures US 2000–2009: 3-fold increase; $720 million/year in 2009 — early database documentation of opioid epidemic's neonatal impact.
Tolia VN, Clark RH. Children 2020;7(11):216: 'The denominator matters! Lessons from large database research in neonatology' — essential reading for understanding database study methodology.
Edwards EM et al. Transl Pediatr 2019;8:182: Vermont Oxford Network: worldwide learning community — how VON enables collaborative QI across >1,000 NICUs globally.
Mohamed TL et al. (PHIS nephrotoxins): 192,229 neonates: 74% exposed ≥1 nephrotoxic medication → significantly higher AKI — highlights burden of nephrotoxin exposure via administrative database.
Bloom BT et al. (QI + growth): CQI project → HC improvement 0.2 cm — statistically significant but clinically meaningless; classic example of big data paradox.
Dobson et al. (survivor bias caffeine): early caffeine <24 wk appeared higher mortality — survivor bias artifact; infants in late group had already survived early high-mortality period.
13. Self-Check Q&A
Q: Neonatal database research ทำอะไรได้ที่ RCT ทำไม่ได้ — ยกตัวอย่าง 3 อย่าง?

ตอบ: (1) Disease incidence + emerging diseases: NAS incidence ↑ 3.4x (Tolia NEJM 2015) — impossible to RCT. (2) Adverse event detection: cefotaxime → fungal sepsis + mortality (Clark) — detected from large CDW. (3) Natural experiments: vitamin A shortage 2011 → BPD rates unchanged — no RCT possible. (4) Practice variation: 22–24 wk resuscitation intercenter variation — RCT unethical.
Q: Survivor bias ใน caffeine study คืออะไร และเหตุใดจึงทำให้ผลดูผิดไปจากความเป็นจริง?

ตอบ: Infants in 'late caffeine' group (received caffeine Day 5) had already survived the high-mortality period (Day 1–5). Infants in 'early caffeine' group were at risk throughout Day 1–5. Late group = artificially selected survivors → apparent lower mortality ≠ drug benefit. Lesson: whenever comparing early vs late treatment in database, always ask 'could this be survivor bias?'
Q: Referral bias ส่งผลต่อการ interpret NICU outcome data อย่างไร? ยก NEC เป็นตัวอย่าง?

ตอบ: Quaternary NICUs receive referred cases of surgical NEC; ดังนั้น NEC outcomes ที่ quaternary centers will appear worse than community NICUs — but this reflects higher severity/complexity of referred cases, NOT worse quality of care. Invalid to compare NEC mortality between surgical and non-surgical NICUs without correcting for referral bias.
Q: NICHD Outcome Estimator คืออะไร ใช้ variables อะไร และ limitation หลักคืออะไร?

ตอบ: Risk calculator for in-hospital + 2-year outcomes ในทารก extremely preterm. Variables: GA, BW, sex, singleton/multiple, antenatal steroids. Built from large NRN registry. Limitation: derived from US NRN academic centers → may not be calibrated for Thai/KCMH population; does not account for individual center outcomes variation.
Q: ทำไม p < 0.05 ใน database study อาจ ไม่ clinically meaningful? ยกตัวอย่าง?

ตอบ: Large N → even tiny differences become statistically significant. NICHD example: 1-year maternal age difference — statistically significant but clinically irrelevant. Bloom et al.: HC +0.2 cm — within measurement error, not clinically meaningful. Rule: always evaluate effect size + absolute difference + NNT — not just p-value.
Q: เหตุใด cefotaxime use ลดลงใน US NICUs ใน early 2000s และ database research มีบทบาทอย่างไร?

ตอบ: Pediatrix CDW signal (Clark et al.): cefotaxime → ↑ mortality + ↑ fungal sepsis across all GA groups. Following this database signal: QI + educational efforts → cefotaxime use ↓ dramatically → survival improved + fungal sepsis ↓. This is the prototypical example of database-derived adverse signal → practice change → outcome improvement. Fig 3.1 ของ chapter แสดง timeline ชัดเจน.
Q: Denominator ใน database study: ทำไม outborn infants ต้องถูก include เมื่อศึกษา therapeutic hypothermia?

ตอบ: Many HIE infants are outborn (transferred from community hospitals). Excluding outborn → artificially low HIE rates → underestimates true event rate + burden of disease. Tolia & Clark 2020: 'The denominator matters!' — inclusion/exclusion choices directly drive validity.
Q: Propensity score matching ต่างจาก multivariate logistic regression อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Logistic regression: adjusts for confounders mathematically within the analysis. Propensity score matching: calculates probability of receiving treatment based on patient characteristics → matches treated/untreated patients with similar propensity scores → mimics randomization before analysis. Both reduce selection bias but propensity matching more closely mimics RCT structure; useful when RCT would be difficult to perform.

Day 101 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.4
บทต่อไป: Chapter 4 — Medical Ethics in Neonatal Care (Fanaroff 12th ed.). เตรียมตัว: 4 ethical principles (autonomy, beneficence, nonmaleficence, justice), periviable counseling framework, withholding/withdrawing care, brain death in neonates, research ethics — เชื่อมต่อกับ database practice variation ที่เรียนวันนี้โดยตรง (จริยธรรมของการ treat vs not treat ที่ 22–24 wk).
